module_mood_contract: practitioner fluency — 'I know how to test, I know how to learn, I know how to encode' — forward-tilt, flywheel as runnable move not slogan (M6 closing-chain lecture)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands — 8+/10 per teaching beat; below 7 = REVISE.
rules_evaluated.evidence: trace.phases[1].mood_score=9, trace.close.mood_score=9. Single teaching phase (lecture has no ## Phase headers). The Ralph section added 2026-05-15 is a TEACHING beat (names practitioner posture + practitioners-see-levers-first pattern) — mood-floor applies; scores 8/10 locally (mild anthropomorphism in 'the platform watched' is in-bounds for AE101 Claude Code-canonical frame per maintainer source-verification note at line 76). No beat below 8.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — check module N+1 teaching moment.
rules_evaluated.evidence: This lecture is the very last teaching beat in M6 and the core training. No downstream module teaching moment to front-run for core-only cohorts. For M7+ cohorts, M7 opens on team deliberation — flywheel + collaboration framing primes that without resolving M7's own teaching moment.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands — reliably triggers across reasonable persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Two teaching moments: (1) flywheel-as-collaboration-not-autonomy in '## What this is not' — load-bearing carve-out in a named section, not buried in body prose; hard to skip for any delivery mode. (2) Practitioners-see-levers-first in '## Ralph' — Huntley shell hack + /goal landing as recognition. Both section headers are navigational anchors; teaching beats open each section. Reliable across mid-level, senior, and fast-operator personas.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — file honors module Big Idea and mood contract.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M6 Big Idea per strategy doc: compounding engineering, 'spot gaps, build the loop.' Lecture enacts it: flywheel = compounding; agent proposes next iteration = spot gaps; student decides which proposals earn their place = build the loop. Mood contract 'practitioner fluency' honored at close: 'The training closes. The flywheel does not.' 'What this is not' beat explicitly guards against autonomous-agent fantasy drift.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Placement: Module 6 closer chain — Human close → The loop has a name → Agents that build agents → Next. Maintainer block confirms 'closer-shaped. Lands as the very last teaching beat in Module 6.' Names the flywheel pattern after the student has authored two skills — told after: recognition, not abstract claim.
No contrast-mood exercises follow this lecture in M6 or in the core training. Rule applies to openers before contrast-mood modules (M5-style); this is a terminal closer.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages room outside text — no authored conversation cues.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'share with your neighbor,' 'tell the room,' 'take 90 seconds and compare,' or equivalent room-management sentences present. Body stays on the work (flywheel move, prompt to try, Ralph pattern). After-cohort prompt framed as optional ('on a quiet half-hour') — student agency, not a trainer cue.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: One fenced prompt block ({{prompt:agents-that-build-agents-handoff}}) carries the forcing function (plan mode + three design questions). Body around it ('Read what comes back the way you read any plan. Push back where the proposals miss…') is post-prompt follow-on guidance — not a forcing function in body prose. Correctly layered.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — close hands off to next module open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: For core-only cohorts: terminal close, no handoff needed; 'The training closes. The flywheel does not.' is the intended landing. For M7+ cohorts: flywheel + 'agents that build agents' framing primes M7's team deliberation — agents arrive at M7 as first-class participants on a shared runtime (per strategy doc M7 section). Practitioner-fluency mood at M6 close is what makes M7's deliberation feel earned.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lecture UX — operator can follow without re-reading prior module.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No prompts requiring external file state beyond what the student has in their kit at M6 close. The fenced prompt assumes plan mode + evidence on disk (M4 un-packaged artefacts, M5 packaged artefacts, second skill) — all exist by M6 close by training design. Minor ambiguity: 'the second skill you just shipped' relies on scrollback or kit memory for the skill's name (flagged in trace.ambiguous_instructions); maintainer open items already flag this for next pass. Not a structural UX blocker.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: If self-study reader pauses between sessions, add explicit path reference: 'the skill in ~/.claude/skills/ you authored at Module 6'.
